answers.higher_rated_fighters_win_more: Yes. OctagonRank favorites won 60.5% of 1282 tested fights since this backtest start date.
answers.octagonrank_vs_raw_baseline: OctagonRank beat the raw Elo-only baseline by 1.9% accuracy points on this sample.
answers.octagonrank_vs_media_rankings: Not proven yet. A fair comparison needs dated media-ranking snapshots from before each fight; current rankings would leak future information.
answers.title_context_check: Title-context fights in the sample tested at 45.6% favorite accuracy across 46 fights.

primary.label: OctagonRank context model
primary.description: Context-aware pre-fight probability using rating, opponent quality, and fight-context adjustments.

baselines.label: Raw Elo-only baseline
baselines.description: Same fight sample using only pre-fight Elo rating gap before context adjustments.

baselines.label: Coin-flip baseline
baselines.description: A naive benchmark: every fight is treated as 50/50.

deltas.label: OctagonRank context model vs Raw Elo-only baseline
OctagonRank context model vs Coin-flip baseline

media_rankings.detail: Current media rankings cannot be used as a fair historical predictor. Add dated media-ranking snapshots before each event to compare OctagonRank against media rankings.

meta_rankings.detail: Current Meta rankings are useful for the live comparison board, but backtesting requires dated snapshots from before each fight.

title_context_validation.interpretation: This checks whether title-context fighters and title-lineage wins behave sensibly in historical fight prediction. It is not a title-shot correctness test by itself.
